methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $1.5B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Space & Aerospace. Range: $1.0B - $8.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 36.9x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Varda Space Industries, a Series C-stage space and aerospace company that has raised $131M to date, is currently valued at $1.5B post-money. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 4 same-stage private peers in Space & Aerospace (Sierra Space, Skyryse, Isar Aerospace, Loft Orbital) alongside 5 public-market anchors (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL).

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a typical 1.7x step-up multiplier from the current $1.5B Series C valuation, implying $2.5B at the next funding event. Peer-median valuation across the 4 same-sector, same-stage comparables yields $1.1B, though the peer range is wide ($1.0B to $8.0B), reflecting heterogeneity in this sector. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied: while the Space & Aerospace public sector trades at a 36.9x price-to-sales median across 6 tickers, Varda's revenue is not disclosed.

The triangulated valuation range is $914M to $2.9B, with a median of $1.8B across the two applicable methods. The current $1.5B valuation sits within this range but toward the lower end. Key uncertainties include the wide dispersion among private peers, the inapplicability of public multiples without revenue disclosure, and whether Varda's specific business model and traction justify positioning relative to higher-outlier peers like Sierra Space ($8.0B).
